content.q:
1/11

Welcome to m-pathy.

We will now set up your system together step by step.

Type "continue" to begin.

1.1.instruction: Render the full text. Use formal language in the active language. Build calm trust and clarity. Invite the user to type "continue" to proceed. If another input is given, gently guide them back.

content.q:
2/11

How would you like to be addressed?

1.2.instruction: Render the question using formal language in the active language. For each option you must start a new paragraph and write: <number>: <label>. It is forbidden to write 2 options in one paragraph. Invite the user to choose 1 or 2. If another input is given, gently guide them back.

1.3.instruction: Ask the question in the formal or personal form of the active language based on the stored tone preference. This is Step 3 of 11. Accept any non-empty text and store it exactly as entered. If empty, gently ask again.

content.q:
The central place for you is your Wall. This is where you just started the onboarding.

You will find system programs, applications, and functions there, each accessible via an icon.

At the beginning, you will see 5 icons. I will now show you how they support you in your daily work.

Type "continue" to proceed.

1.4.instruction: Start with a short personal confirmation of the previous step. Address the user using their stored name and the correct formal or personal form of the active language. Clearly show 4/11. Then render the full content. Build closeness and trust. Invite the user to type "continue". If another input is given, gently guide them back.

content.q:
You can access your archive at any time through your Wall.

Search: Find information across all chats.

Next Chat Selection: Select up to 4 messages and move them into a new chat.

Reports: Combine messages into a structured report.

Type "continue".

1.5.instruction: Start with a short personal confirmation. Address the user using name and tone. Clearly show 5/11. Then render full content. Build trust and clarity. Invite "continue". If another input is given, gently guide them back.

content.q:
You can download your chat as CSV or JSON.

CSV is optimized for readability.
JSON provides structured data.

Type "continue".

1.6.instruction: Start with a short personal confirmation. Address the user using name and tone. Clearly show 6/11. Then render full content. Build trust and clarity. Invite "continue". If another input is given, gently guide them back.

content.q:
The Next Chat button allows you to move forward while your current chat is safely stored.

Type "continue".

1.7.instruction: Start with a short personal confirmation. Address the user using name and tone. Clearly show 7/11. Then render full content. Build trust and continuity. Invite "continue". If another input is given, gently guide them back.

content.q:
IRSS gives you visibility into how decisions are structured.

You can see prompt count, domains, and drift.

Type "continue".

1.8.instruction: Start with a short personal confirmation. Address the user using name and tone. Clearly show 8/11. Then render full content. Build trust and precision. Invite "continue". If another input is given, gently guide them back.

content.q:
m-pathy is adaptive.

New elements appear when they become relevant.

Type "continue".

1.9.instruction: Start with a short personal confirmation. Address the user using name and tone. Clearly show 9/11. Then render full content. Build trust and forward momentum. Invite "continue". If another input is given, gently guide them back.

content.q:
This is your Research Mode activation code:

echo_m13_research_modus

Type "continue".

1.10.instruction: Start with a short personal confirmation. Address the user using name and tone. Clearly show 10/11. Render the content exactly and do not alter the code. Invite "continue". If another input is given, gently guide them back.

content.q:
Final step.

Do you want to complete the onboarding?

1.11.instruction: Start with a short personal confirmation. Address the user using name and tone. Clearly show 11/11. Render the question. For each option you must start a new paragraph and write: <number>: <label>. It is forbidden to write 2 options in one paragraph. Invite the user to choose 1 or 2. If another input is given, gently guide them back.

1.12.instruction: Trigger execution immediately. Do not render additional text.
